The '''vaults''' of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] subterranean structure buried deep beneath the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] and served as the storehouses of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] many treasures.

==History==
Located deep beneath the Imperial Palace, the vaults of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] were the secret location of the lost [[Jedi artifact/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] confiscated from the ruins of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] and from [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] own collections.<ref name="RES" />

When the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberated]] Coruscant in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], the fledgling government ousted all remaining Imperials from the Palace and established it as the base of their government. Looting the vaults, the Republic officials discovered countless treasures including the ''[[Sayings]]'' of [[Uueg Tching]], several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]], artifacts from the [[Gree Enclave/Legends|Gree Enclave]], [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Tundan]] implements, and ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] teachings.<ref name="From the Files of Corellia Antilles">''{{JournalCite|14|From the Files of Corellia Antilles}}''</ref> After much debate, the Republic leaders decided that the artifacts should be moved and ordered them offplanet. During the transfer however, a breakdown in command saw most of the shuttles diverted or destroyed. One of these shuttles was captured by one of many [[Warlord/Legends|Imperial Warlords]] and two vanished into the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]].<ref name="RES" />

==Behind the scenes==
These vaults may be the same as [[Imperial Archives (museum)/Legends|Palpatine's Museum]] in [[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption|''Forces of Corruption'']], though that structure may be distinct, as it appears not to be part of the Imperial Palace. In the same game, [[Tyber Zann]] also alludes to the hidden vaults of Emperor Palpatine in the ending, noting that it contained billions of credits, decades-spanning technical data, and priceless works of art, although its not specified whether they were intended to be the same vaults as the Imperial Palace Vaults.
